rule_name: Strategy Code Executed With Full Python Privileges (No Sandbox)
fix_guidance: Implement RestrictedPython sandbox or at minimum audit strategy code before loading. Consider subprocess isolation for strategy execution.
note: iresolver.py uses importlib.util.spec_from_file_location() + exec_module() to load user strategy files. This executes arbitrary Python with full access to exchange API keys, filesystem, and network. By design but constitutes a significant trust boundary.

rule_name: FreqAI Dynamic Module Import with User-Controlled Name
fix_guidance: Validate model_type and rl_config.model_type against an allowlist before calling importlib.import_module().
note: data_drawer.py L608-611: importlib.import_module(self.model_type, self.freqai_info['rl_config']['model_type']) with user-configurable values. Could import arbitrary modules.

rule_name: Float Precision in Financial Order Amounts
fix_guidance: Use decimal.Decimal for amount and rate parameters. CCXT handles exchange-side precision but internal calculations accumulate float errors.
note: create_order() signature uses float for amount and rate. In high-frequency or large-volume trading, IEEE 754 float precision errors could cause order rejections or slippage.

rule_name: Telegram RPC Exposes Force-Trade Operations Remotely
fix_guidance: Add confirmation prompt with trade details before executing force_enter/force_exit. Consider requiring admin PIN for sensitive operations.
note: _force_enter (L1512), _force_exit (L1404), _stop (L1357), _reload_config (L1369) all accessible via Telegram. While Telegram auth protects this, a compromised Telegram account could execute trades remotely.

rule_name: No Hard Max Position Size Enforcement Before Order
fix_guidance: Add explicit max_position_size check in create_order(). Do not rely solely on exchange-side validation.
note: create_order() calls self._api.create_order() without validating amount against configured max_position or total exposure. Exchange-level limits are the only guard.

rule_name: Trade State Transition Without Locking in Async Context
fix_guidance: Use asyncio.Lock around trade state mutations. Concurrent Telegram commands and strategy signals could race on trade state.
note: trade_model.py mutates trade state (open_rate, stop_loss, etc.) without explicit locking. In async context with Telegram RPC + strategy signals, concurrent modifications are possible.

rule_name: CCXT Rate Limiting Handled but Retry Logic Inconsistent
fix_guidance: Ensure consistent exponential backoff + jitter across all exchange implementations. Some exchange subclasses may not retry on DDoSProtection.
note: DDoSProtection caught at L1496 but retry logic varies per exchange subclass. Binance handles it well; smaller exchanges may not.
